rules.title: Secure System Configuration
rules.description: Do not use vendor-supplied defaults for system passwords and security parameters
rules.remediation: Remove all default passwords. Use environment variables for configuration. Document secure configuration standards.

rules.title: Protect Stored Cardholder Data
rules.description: Protect stored cardholder data through encryption and other security measures
rules.remediation: Encrypt all cardholder data at rest using strong cryptography (AES-256). Implement key management procedures.

rules.title: Encrypt Transmission of Cardholder Data
rules.description: Encrypt transmission of cardholder data across open, public networks
rules.remediation: Use TLS 1.2+ for all cardholder data transmissions. Disable insecure protocols (SSL, TLS 1.0/1.1).

rules.title: Secure Development Practices
rules.description: Develop and maintain secure systems and applications
rules.remediation: Implement secure coding practices. Use automated security testing in CI/CD pipeline. Conduct regular code reviews.

rules.title: Identify and Authenticate Access
rules.description: Assign a unique ID to each person with computer access
rules.remediation: Implement unique user IDs, strong authentication, and multi-factor authentication for all system access.

rules.title: Track and Monitor Network Access
rules.description: Track and monitor all access to network resources and cardholder data
rules.remediation: Implement comprehensive logging of all access to cardholder data. Include user identification, timestamps, and actions.
